description:
The visual system. Not 'a nice-looking chart' — a set of measurable rules for encoding data without lying about it.

Colour-map CLASS is selected from declared data semantics, never from a skill name and never from fashion: sequential for ordered magnitude, diverging only when a meaningful reference centre exists, cyclic for phase, categorical for unordered groups. A diverging request with no centre is REFUSED, because a diverging map implies a symmetry the data may not have.

Categorical identity is never carried by hue alone. Every series style is a TUPLE of colour, dash pattern, and marker shape, so the figure survives greyscale printing and colour-vision deficiency.

colormaps.citation: Crameri, F. (2018). Geodynamic diagnostics, scientific visualisation and StagLab 3.0. Geoscientific Model Development.
usedFor: ordered magnitude: delays, rates, densities, weights with no meaningful zero
signed values with a MEANINGFUL zero: excitatory/inhibitory weights, potentiation/depression
colormaps.note: Refused without an explicit centre: RENDER_DIVERGING_SCALE_NO_CENTER.

usedFor: phase and angle, where 0 and 2*pi are the same value

prohibited: Rainbow / jet as a default. Its lightness is non-monotonic, so it manufactures visual boundaries the data does not contain and hides real ones.
Colour as the SOLE categorical cue.
A diverging map with no meaningful centre.
A sequential map for unordered categories.
Mapping MISSING to zero, or to the minimum colour. Missing is a distinct state and gets a distinct legend key.
Arbitrary caller CSS or style objects anywhere in the normative scientific output path.

categoricalSeries.source: Okabe and Ito (2008), colour-vision-deficiency safe qualitative palette
categoricalSeries.beyondCapacity: Small multiples, explicit grouping, or a typed refusal (RENDER_SERIES_LIMIT_EXCEEDED). Cortexel does not mint dozens of indistinguishable colours and call it a legend.
categoricalSeries.note: Each style is a TUPLE. Two series remain distinguishable by dash pattern and marker shape with colour removed entirely — a test asserts exactly that, which is what makes the figure survive a greyscale printout.

contrastTargets.testedAgainst: The FINAL token pairs actually used, not isolated palette swatches. A colour that passes in isolation can still fail on the background it is drawn on.
contrastTargets.scope: These targets apply to the surfaces CORTEXEL OWNS. Cortexel makes no conformance claim about the host page it is embedded in — that would be a claim it has no way to support.

uncertaintyStyles.label: {level}% {coverage} {method} confidence interval (over {basis}, n = {sampleCount})
uncertaintyStyles.note: The legend states exactly what is drawn. It never says merely 'error'.

uncertaintyStyles.label: {level}% {coverage} {method} credible interval (over {basis}, n = {sampleCount})
uncertaintyStyles.note: Diagnostic vocabulary only in contract 1.0. Every stable skill refuses this kind; no attestation-verification boundary exists.

uncertaintyStyles.label: +/-1 SD (n = {sampleCount}, over {basis})
uncertaintyStyles.note: A dispersion, drawn as a whisker. It is NOT an interval and is never relabelled as one.

uncertaintyStyles.label: observed min-max across {basis} (n = {sampleCount})
uncertaintyStyles.note: Carries NO coverage probability. Never drawn or captioned as a confidence interval.

uncertaintyStyles.label: no uncertainty shown ({reason})
uncertaintyStyles.note: Rendered as an explicit statement. The renderer never invents a ribbon to fill the space.
